commands.result_summary: Launching skill: tmp:uipath-maestro-bpmn

1	# Structural BPMN (what the registry does not emit)
2	
3	The registry's `xmlTemplate`s give you the `uipath:*` payload for each node
4	(see [registry-workflow.md](registry-workflow.md)). They do **not** give you the
5	structural BPMN that holds those nodes together. This file is the ground truth
6	for everything you author by hand around the templates.
7	
8	Two sources define the contract:
9	
10	- the registry spec `bpmn-spec.json` — enumerates which BPMN element types and
11	  event definitions exist, via its `bpmnElements` section;
12	- the Studio Web canvas serializer
13	  (`PO.Frontend/src/services/serialization/`) — defines how that XML must be
14	  shaped to import and round-trip.
15	
16	Where the registry stops, the canvas serializer is authoritative. Each
17	gap below is labelled **REGISTRY GAP** — the registry exposes no template for
18	it, so author it from this reference.
19	
20	## The document scaffold (REGISTRY GAP)
21	
22	The registry emits no `<bpmn:definitions>` / `<bpmn:process>` root and no
23	namespace declarations. Author this shell yourself. The canvas import detector
24	(`exporter.ts`) requires: a root `<…:definitions>` carrying a BPMN-spec
25	namespace, at least one `<bpmn:process>`, and (to render) a
26	`<bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>`.
27	
28	```xml
29	<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
30	<bpmn:definitions
31	    xmlns:bpmn="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL"
32	    xmlns:bpmndi="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/DI"
33	    xmlns:dc="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DC"
34	    xmlns:di="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DI"
35	    xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
36	    xmlns:uipath="http://uipath.org/schema/bpmn"
37	    id="Definitions_1" targetNamespace="http://bpmn.io/schema/bpmn"
38	    exporter="UiPath (https://bpmn.uipath.com)" exporterVersion="1.0">
39	  <bpmn:process id="Process_1" isExecutable="true">
40	    <!-- variables, flow nodes, sequence flows -->
41	  </bpmn:process>
42	  <bpmndi:BPMNDiagram id="Diagram_1">
43	    <bpmndi:BPMNPlane id="Plane_1" bpmnElement="Process_1">
44	      <!-- one BPMNShape per node, one BPMNEdge per flow -->
45	    </bpmndi:BPMNPlane>
46	  </bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>
47	</bpmn:definitions>
48	```
49	
50	The `uipath:` namespace URI is exactly `http://uipath.org/schema/bpmn`. All
51	`uipath:*` tags inside `extensionElements` are lower-camelCase
52	(`uipath:activity`, `uipath:variables`, `uipath:loopCharacteristics`, …).
53	
54	Every `uipath:activity` / `uipath:event` / `uipath:mapping` carries its node
55	type as a **child** element — `<uipath:type value="<Type>" version="v1" />` —
56	never as a `type=` attribute on the wrapper. This holds both for templated nodes
57	and for any shell you author or preserve by hand.
58	
59	XML comments must not contain `--` (double-hyphen): it is invalid XML and the
60	file will fail to parse. Never paste CLI commands or flags
61	(`--output`, `--connection-id`) into `<!-- … -->`. Keep comments minimal.
62	
63	## A complete minimal file (author from this, not from fixtures)
64	
65	This is the whole shape — variables, an entry point, one node, a branch, and
66	the diagram — in one valid file. Author from this skeleton plus the registry
67	templates for your nodes. **Do not read the validator's `test/fixtures/` to
68	infer the pattern**; those are validator test data, and reading them is the
69	main reason authoring runs out of time. Swap the `scriptTask` payload for the
70	registry `xmlTemplate` of whatever node you need.
71	
72	```xml
73	<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
74	<bpmn:definitions
75	    xmlns:bpmn="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL"
76	    xmlns:bpmndi="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/DI"
77	    xmlns:dc="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DC"
78	    xmlns:di="http://www.omg.org/spec/DD/20100524/DI"
79	    xmlns:uipath="http://uipath.org/schema/bpmn"
80	    id="Definitions_1" targetNamespace="http://bpmn.io/schema/bpmn"
81	    exporter="UiPath (https://bpmn.uipath.com)" exporterVersion="1.0">
82	  <bpmn:process id="Process_1" isExecutable="true">
83	    <bpmn:extensionElements>
84	      <uipath:migrationVersion version="11.5" />
85	      <uipath:variables version="v1">
86	        <uipath:inputOutput id="Var_Amount" name="Amount" type="number" />
87	        <uipath:inputOutput id="Var_Tier" name="Tier" type="string" />
88	      </uipath:variables>
89	    </bpmn:extensionElements>
90	    <bpmn:startEvent id="Start_1" name="Start"><bpmn:outgoing>Flow_1</bpmn:outgoing></bpmn:startEvent>
91	    <bpmn:scriptTask id="Task_Tier" name="Classify" scriptFormat="JavaScript">
92	      <bpmn:extensionElements>
93	        <uipath:scriptVersion value="v3" />
94	        <uipath:mapping version="v1">
95	          <uipath:type value="BPMN.ScriptTask" version="v1" />
96	          <uipath:input name="args"><![CDATA[{"amount":"=vars.Var_Amount"}]]></uipath:input>
97	          <uipath:output name="tier" type="string" var="Var_Tier" source="=result.response" />
98	        </uipath:mapping>
99	      </bpmn:extensionElements>
100	      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_1</bpmn:incoming><bpmn:outgoing>Flow_2</bpmn:outgoing>
101	      <bpmn:script><![CDATA[return { response: amount > 1000 ? "high" : "low" };]]></bpmn:script>
102	    </bpmn:scriptTask>
103	    <bpmn:exclusiveGateway id="Gw_1" name="Tier?" default="Flow_Low">
104	      <bpmn:incoming>Flow_2</bpmn:incoming>
105	      <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_High</bpmn:outgoing><bpmn:outgoing>Flow_Low</bpmn:outgoing>
106	    </bpmn:exclusiveGateway>
107	    <bpmn:endEvent id="End_High" name="High"><bpmn:incoming>Flow_High</bpmn:incoming></bpmn:endEvent>
108	    <bpmn:endEvent id="End_Low" name="Low"><bpmn:incoming>Flow_Low</bpmn:incoming></bpmn:endEvent>
109	    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_1" sourceRef="Start_1" targetRef="Task_Tier" />
110	    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_2" sourceRef="Task_Tier" targetRef="Gw_1" />
111	    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_High" sourceRef="Gw_1" targetRef="End_High">
112	      <bpmn:conditionExpression xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression"
113	        xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">=vars.Var_Tier == "high"</bpmn:conditionExpression>
114	    </bpmn:sequenceFlow>
115	    <bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_Low" sourceRef="Gw_1" targetRef="End_Low" />
116	  </bpmn:process>
117	  <bpmndi:BPMNDiagram id="Diagram_1">
118	    <bpmndi:BPMNPlane id="Plane_1" bpmnElement="Process_1">
119	      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_Start" bpmnElement="Start_1"><dc:Bounds x="160" y="100" width="36" height="36" /></bpmndi:BPMNShape>
120	      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_Task" bpmnElement="Task_Tier"><dc:Bounds x="250" y="78" width="100" height="80" /></bpmndi:BPMNShape>
121	      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_Gw" bpmnElement="Gw_1"><dc:Bounds x="410" y="93" width="50" height="50" /></bpmndi:BPMNShape>
122	      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_High" bpmnElement="End_High"><dc:Bounds x="520" y="40" width="36" height="36" /></bpmndi:BPMNShape>
123	      <bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_Low" bpmnElement="End_Low"><dc:Bounds x="520" y="160" width="36" height="36" /></bpmndi:BPMNShape>
124	      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="E_1" bpmnElement="Flow_1"><di:waypoint x="196" y="118" /><di:waypoint x="250" y="118" /></bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
125	      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="E_2" bpmnElement="Flow_2"><di:waypoint x="350" y="118" /><di:waypoint x="410" y="118" /></bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
126	      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="E_High" bpmnElement="Flow_High"><di:waypoint x="435" y="93" /><di:waypoint x="435" y="58" /><di:waypoint x="520" y="58" /></bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
127	      <bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="E_Low" bpmnElement="Flow_Low"><di:waypoint x="435" y="143" /><di:waypoint x="435" y="178" /><di:waypoint x="520" y="178" /></bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
128	    </bpmndi:BPMNPlane>
129	  </bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>
130	</bpmn:definitions>
131	```
132	
133	## Variables (`BPMN.Variables`)
134	
135	Declare root variables with the `BPMN.Variables` registry template attached to
136	the process via `extensionElements`, or use the canvas `<uipath:variables>`
137	block directly. Variable bodies are CDATA. Reference variables in expressions as
138	`vars.<id>` — see [expression-authoring.md](expression-authoring.md).
139	Sub-process-scoped variables go in that sub-process's own `<uipath:variables>`.
140	
141	## Script tasks (`BPMN.ScriptTask`) — Jint runtime contract
142	
143	`bpmn:scriptTask scriptFormat="JavaScript"` runs under **Jint**, not Node.js or
144	a browser. The mapping payload comes from the `BPMN.ScriptTask` registry
145	template, but the runtime contract is fixed:
146	
147	- Only these helpers exist: `uipath.aggregate`, `uipath._aggregate`,
148	  `uipath._pipe`, and a no-op `console`. No npm packages, filesystem, network,
149	  browser globals, or long-running async behavior. Execution envelope is ~64 MB
150	  / 30 s.
151	- Set `uipath:scriptVersion value="v3"` for new scripts; preserve an imported
152	  `value="v2"`. For v2+ the script returns JSON under `response`.
153	- Mapped `args` fields are read as **top-level identifiers** in the script body
154	  (`amount`, not `args.amount`); the input mapping itself stays `name="args"`
155	  and maps each field by variable id (`=vars.Var_Amount`).
156	- Map the return back through `source="=result.response"` (scalar) or
157	  `source="=result.response.<field>"` (object field); `var` points at a declared
158	  variable id (do not put the target id in `name`).
159	
160	```xml
161	<bpmn:scriptTask id="Task_RiskScore" name="Risk Score" scriptFormat="JavaScript">
162	  <bpmn:extensionElements>
163	    <uipath:scriptVersion value="v3" />
164	    <uipath:mapping version="v1">
165	      <uipath:type value="BPMN.ScriptTask" version="v1" />
166	      <uipath:input name="args"><![CDATA[{"amount":"=vars.Var_Amount","daysOverdue":"=vars.Var_DaysOverdue"}]]></uipath:input>
167	      <uipath:output name="riskScore" type="number" var="Var_RiskScore" source="=result.response" />
168	    </uipath:mapping>
169	  </bpmn:extensionElements>
170	  <bpmn:script><![CDATA[
171	var score = amount * 0.01 + daysOverdue * 2;
172	return { response: score };
173	]]></bpmn:script>
174	</bpmn:scriptTask>
175	```
176	
177	## Sequence flows, conditions, and gateway defaults (REGISTRY GAP)
178	
179	The registry never emits `<bpmn:sequenceFlow>`, conditions, or the gateway
180	`default` attribute. Author all of them.
181	
182	- A flow: `<bpmn:sequenceFlow id="Flow_1" sourceRef="A" targetRef="B" />`. The
183	  source/target nodes must also list `<bpmn:incoming>`/`<bpmn:outgoing>`
184	  (the registry templates leave `{incomingEdge}`/`{outgoingEdge}` placeholders
185	  for exactly these).
186	- Conditional flow body: `<bpmn:conditionExpression xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression">=vars.Var_X == "approved"</bpmn:conditionExpression>`.
187	  The canvas normalizes the body to start with `=` — always lead with `=`.
188	- Gateway default flow: set `default="Flow_else"` on the gateway element, and
189	  give that flow no condition.
190	
191	## Gateways
192	
193	Author these gateway types for new BPMN: `bpmn:ExclusiveGateway`,
194	`bpmn:ParallelGateway`, `bpmn:InclusiveGateway`, `bpmn:EventBasedGateway`.
195	`bpmn:ComplexGateway` round-trips structurally but is **preserve-only** — do not
196	generate it for new authoring (see [Do not generate for new
197	authoring](#do-not-generate-for-new-authoring-preserve-on-round-trip-only)).
198	
199	- **Exclusive (XOR)**: each non-default outgoing flow needs a
200	  `conditionExpression`; exactly one outgoing flow is the `default`. (Validator
201	  rule `MISSING_CONDITION_EXPRESSION`.)
202	- **Parallel (AND)**: fork = one in, many out; join = many in, one out. No
203	  conditions.
204	- **Inclusive (OR)**: conditions on outgoing flows; multiple may be taken.
205	- **Event-based**: routes to the first of several catch events / receive tasks
206	  to fire; its outgoing flows target intermediate catch events or receive tasks.
207	- A gateway with exactly one incoming and one outgoing flow is rejected
208	  (`SUPERFLUOUS_GATEWAY`). Activities/events must not have more than one
209	  incoming flow — join with a gateway, not a "fake join" (`FAKE_JOIN`).
210	
211	## Events and the event-definition matrix
212	
213	`bpmn-spec.json` `bpmnElements.events` enumerates which event definitions each
214	event element can carry on **round-trip**. For **new authoring**, only the
215	**none**, **Message**, **Timer**, **Error** (on end + boundary), and
216	**Terminate** (on end events only) definitions are generated. Conditional,
217	Signal, Escalation, Compensate, Cancel, Link, multiple, and parallel-multiple
218	definitions are **preserve-only** — keep them when imported, but do not generate
219	them for new BPMN (see [Do not generate for new
220	authoring](#do-not-generate-for-new-authoring-preserve-on-round-trip-only)).
221	
222	The matrix below is the round-trip acceptance per element; **preserve-only**
223	marks definitions that the skill keeps but does not author for new files.
224	
225	| Event element | Authorable | Preserve-only (round-trip) |
226	| --- | --- | --- |
227	| `bpmn:StartEvent` | none, Message, Timer | Conditional, Signal |
228	| `bpmn:IntermediateThrowEvent` | none, Message | Escalation, Signal, Link, Compensate |
229	| `bpmn:IntermediateCatchEvent` | Message, Timer | Escalation, Signal, Conditional, Link, Compensate |
230	| `bpmn:EndEvent` | none, Message, Error, Terminate | Escalation, Compensate, Signal |
231	| `bpmn:BoundaryEvent` | Message, Timer, Error | Escalation, Conditional, Signal, Compensate |
232	
233	Payload shapes the canvas serializes:
234	
235	- **Timer**: `<bpmn:timerEventDefinition><bpmn:timeDuration xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression">PT30M</bpmn:timeDuration></bpmn:timerEventDefinition>`
236	  (or `timeDate` / `timeCycle`). Static durations must be valid ISO-8601;
237	  week designators (`PnW`) are unsupported. Expression-mode is allowed and
238	  accepts either prefix — `=…` or `@…`.
239	- **Message**: `<bpmn:messageEventDefinition messageRef="Message_1" />` with a
240	  `<bpmn:message id="Message_1" name="…"/>` declared at definitions level. The
241	  Maestro internal-message events (`Maestro.ReceiveMessageEvent` /
242	  `Maestro.SendMessageEvent`) carry the `uipath:event` payload **and** a bare
243	  `<bpmn:messageEventDefinition />` (see their registry templates).
244	- **Error**: `<bpmn:errorEventDefinition errorRef="Error_1" />` with a
245	  `<bpmn:error id="Error_1" name="…" errorCode="…"/>` at definitions level. An
246	  error end event with no `errorRef` fails to parse at runtime
247	  (`ERROR_END_EVENT_MISSING_EXCEPTION`); an error referenced by a boundary event
248	  must declare an `errorCode` (`ERROR_BOUNDARY_EVENT_REQUIRES_ERROR_CODE`).
249	- **Terminate** (end events only): emit the bare
250	  `<bpmn:terminateEventDefinition />`.
251	
252	Preserve-only payloads — keep these when imported, but do not author them for
253	new files:
254	
255	- **Signal**: `<bpmn:signalEventDefinition signalRef="Signal_1" />` with a
256	  definitions-level `<bpmn:signal/>`.
257	- **Escalation**: `<bpmn:escalationEventDefinition escalationRef="Escalation_1" />`
258	  with a `<bpmn:escalation id="Escalation_1" name="…" escalationCode="…"/>`
259	  declared at definitions level (parallel to message/error/signal).
260	- **Conditional / Link / Compensate**: the bare definition element; the canvas
261	  round-trips it.
262	
263	### Boundary events (REGISTRY GAP for `attachedToRef` / `cancelActivity`)
264	
265	A boundary event attaches to an activity and catches an event on it. The
266	registry exposes no boundary template; author it:
267	
268	```xml
269	<bpmn:boundaryEvent id="Boundary_Timeout" attachedToRef="Task_DoWork" cancelActivity="true">
270	  <bpmn:timerEventDefinition>
271	    <bpmn:timeDuration xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression">PT15M</bpmn:timeDuration>
272	  </bpmn:timerEventDefinition>
273	  <bpmn:outgoing>Flow_OnTimeout</bpmn:outgoing>
274	</bpmn:boundaryEvent>
275	```
276	
277	- `attachedToRef` = id of the activity it sits on. The boundary event and that
278	  activity must be `flowElements` of the same parent scope.
279	- `cancelActivity="true"` = interrupting (default); `cancelActivity="false"` =
280	  non-interrupting. Per the spec, non-interrupting is available for
281	  Message/Timer/Escalation/Conditional/Signal — **not** Error or Compensate.
282	- Only one catch-all (no `errorRef`) error boundary event per task, and no two
283	  error boundary events with the same error code on one task
284	  (`MULTIPLE_CATCH_ALL_BOUNDARY_EVENTS_ON_TASK`,
285	  `DUPLICATE_ERROR_BOUNDARY_EVENT_ON_TASK`).
286	
287	### Retry and error mapping (REGISTRY GAP)
288	
289	UiPath-specific retry and error-mapping metadata live inside an activity's
290	`extensionElements`. The error **code** lives on the declared
291	`bpmn:error errorCode="…"`; `uipath:*` elements reference it through `errorRef`.
292	
293	```xml
294	<uipath:retry maxRetryCount="2" retryBackoff="PT30S" retryBackoffType="exponential"
295	              maxDuration="PT5M" exponentialBase="2" retryAllErrors="false">
296	  <uipath:errorDefinition errorRef="Error_ServiceUnavailable" />
297	</uipath:retry>
298	<uipath:errorMapping version="v1">
299	  <uipath:error id="Mapped_ServiceUnavailable" errorRef="Error_ServiceUnavailable"
300	                priority="1" condition="=vars.error.code == "SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE""
301	                detail="Service unavailable" retryable="true" />
302	</uipath:errorMapping>
303	```
304	
305	- `uipath:retry` attributes: `maxRetryCount`, `retryBackoff`, `retryBackoffType`,
306	  `maxDuration`, `exponentialBase`, `retryAllErrors`. Do not use stale aliases
307	  (`maxAttempts`, `interval`).
308	- `uipath:error` (mapping) fields: `id`, `errorRef`, `priority`, `condition`,
309	  `detail`, `retryable` (`true`/`false`). Conditions read the runtime error via
310	  `vars.error` and contain no assignments. Do not put `code=` on `uipath:error`;
311	  model the code on `bpmn:error errorCode` and reference via `errorRef`.
312	
313	## Subprocess, call activity, event subprocess (REGISTRY GAP for structure)
314	
315	- **SubProcess** (`bpmn:SubProcess`): a container with its own nested
316	  `flowElements` (start event, nodes, end event) and its own scoped
317	  `<uipath:variables>`. Variants: `collapsed`, `expanded`, `eventSubprocess`.
318	  The shape carries `isExpanded` for the collapsed/expanded distinction.
319	- **Event subprocess**: a `bpmn:SubProcess` with `triggeredByEvent="true"`. It
320	  must have **exactly one** start event, and that start event **must carry an
321	  event definition** (with `isInterrupting`) — a blank start event is invalid for
322	  an event subprocess.
323	- **Call activity** (`bpmn:CallActivity`): invokes a *separate* Maestro
324	  instance. The registry provides the `uipath:activity` payload for the
325	  Orchestrator agentic/case-management call-activity types
326	  (`Orchestrator.StartAgenticProcess[Async]`, `…CaseMgmtProcess[Async]`). A
327	  plain BPMN `calledElement` round-trips but is not specially authored by the
328	  canvas layer.
329	- Each scope (process or sub-process) may have at most one blank (untyped) start
330	  event (`MULTIPLE_BLANK_START_EVENTS`).
331	
332	> SubProcess scopes operations *within the same instance*; CallActivity invokes
333	> a *separate* Maestro instance. Do not conflate them.
334	
335	## Multi-instance / loop characteristics (REGISTRY GAP — canvas supports it)
336	
337	The registry spec enumerates **no** multi-instance or loop markers
338	(`grep` for `multiInstance`/`loopCharacteristics` in `bpmn-spec.json` returns
339	nothing). This is a genuine registry gap. The Studio Web canvas, however, **does**
340	serialize them (`elements/nodes.ts`), so author them from the canvas contract:
341	
342	```xml
343	<bpmn:multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics isSequential="true">
344	  <bpmn:completionCondition xsi:type="bpmn:tFormalExpression">=vars.Var_Done</bpmn:completionCondition>
345	  <bpmn:extensionElements>
346	    <uipath:loopCharacteristics
347	        inputCollection="=vars.Var_Items" inputElement="item" />
348	  </bpmn:extensionElements>
349	</bpmn:multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics>
350	```
351	
352	- `isSequential="true"` = one at a time; `false` = parallel.
353	- The collection/item binding lives in the `uipath:loopCharacteristics`
354	  extension (`inputCollection`, `inputElement`), **not** in `loopCardinality`
355	  (the canvas never reads `loopCardinality`).
356	- The loop element **must declare `inputElement`** — do not rely on reading a
357	  bare `iterator`/`iterator.item` downstream without it. For a multi-instance
358	  **subprocess** body, bind `inputElement="iterator[0]"` on
359	  `uipath:loopCharacteristics` and pass the current item into body activities
360	  with `=iterator[0].item`. Do not assume a bare alias such as `=currentItem` is
361	  in scope inside a marker subprocess body unless the file already uses it.
362	- Inside the body, read the current item with the `iterator` namespace — see
363	  [expression-authoring.md](expression-authoring.md).
364	- `bpmn:standardLoopCharacteristics` is also recognized (no uipath extension).
365	
366	Because the registry exposes no template for this, treat it as a documented
367	authoring path backed by the canvas serializer, and tell the user it is a
368	registry gap if they ask why no `registry get` covers it.
369	
370	## Do not generate for new authoring (preserve on round-trip only)
371	
372	These structures are **not** authored for new Maestro BPMN. If they appear in an
373	imported or brownfield file, preserve them and report that the skill cannot
374	safely regenerate or normalize them. Planned / preview / TBD statuses count as
375	unsupported for generation until current tooling confirms them.
376	
377	- Gateway: `bpmn:complexGateway`.
378	- Tasks / containers: `bpmn:manualTask`, `bpmn:adHocSubProcess`,
379	  `bpmn:transaction`.
380	- Event definitions: `conditionalEventDefinition`, `signalEventDefinition`,
381	  `escalationEventDefinition`, `compensateEventDefinition`,
382	  `cancelEventDefinition`, `linkEventDefinition`, multiple, and
383	  parallel-multiple event definitions.
384	- Markers: standard-loop and compensation markers (use only documented
385	  multi-instance parallel/sequential metadata for new loops).
386	- Terminate is supported **only** on end events — not on start, boundary,
387	  intermediate-catch, or intermediate-throw events.
388	- Preserve-only `uipath:*` extension payloads: keep these when imported, and when
389	  a file legitimately needs one, reproduce the shape (with synthetic, public-safe
390	  contents) rather than inventing a new one.
391	  - **Typed activity/event shells always use the lowercase `<uipath:activity>` /
392	    `<uipath:event>` wrapper with a `<uipath:type value="<Type>" version="v1" />`
393	    child** — including types not served by the live registry (e.g.
394	    `Maestro.CasePlanScheduler`, `Maestro.CaseManagerGuardrails`,
395	    `Maestro.CaseRulesEvaluator`). Do **not** substitute the capital-`A`
396	    `<uipath:Activity>` element for a typed shell.
397	  - The capital-`A` `<uipath:Activity>` element is a **separate** generic
398	    preserve-only payload (its own element, not a wrapper for typed shells).
399	  - `uipath:caseManagement` is a versioned body-string element —
400	    `<uipath:caseManagement version="v1">…synthetic payload…</uipath:caseManagement>`.
401	  - `<uipath:scriptVersion value="v2" />` is legacy: author `v3` for new scripts,
402	    preserve `v2` where it already exists.
403	
404	## Diagram interchange — `bpmndi` (REGISTRY GAP — always generated)
405	
406	The registry emits no diagram. Import is **diagram-driven**: the canvas builds
407	nodes from `BPMNShape`s and edges from `BPMNEdge`s, not by walking
408	`flowElements`. **A node with no shape is invisible; a flow with no edge is
409	dropped.** You must generate the full `BPMNDiagram` yourself.
410	
411	- One `<bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_<nodeId>" bpmnElement="<nodeId>">` per node, with
412	  `<dc:Bounds x= y= width= height= />`. SubProcess shapes carry `isExpanded`.
413	- One `<bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="BPMNEdge_<flowId>" bpmnElement="<flowId>">` per
414	  sequence flow, with `<di:waypoint x= y= />` points.
415	- Lay nodes out left-to-right with non-overlapping bounds. Typical sizes: tasks
416	  100×80, events 36×36, gateways 50×50.
417	
418	Example:
419	
420	```xml
421	<bpmndi:BPMNShape id="S_StartEvent_1" bpmnElement="StartEvent_1">
422	  <dc:Bounds x="160" y="100" width="36" height="36" />
423	</bpmndi:BPMNShape>
424	<bpmndi:BPMNEdge id="BPMNEdge_Flow_1" bpmnElement="Flow_1">
425	  <di:waypoint x="196" y="118" />
426	  <di:waypoint x="260" y="118" />
427	</bpmndi:BPMNEdge>
428	```
429	
430	## Editing operations
431	
432	Safe, surgical edits on an existing `.bpmn` (preserve content you did not author
433	— see [SKILL.md](../SKILL.md#editing-an-existing-bpmn-preserve-what-you-did-not-author)):
434	
435	- **Add / delete / reconnect a node**: add the element with a stable id and its
436	  `<bpmn:incoming>`/`<bpmn:outgoing>` refs, add the sequence-flow elements in the
437	  owning scope, and add/update its `BPMNShape` and edge waypoints. On delete,
438	  remove orphaned flows and DI edges and recheck entry-point variables, output
439	  mappings, and binding references.
440	- **Insert a gateway**: split the existing sequence flow into an incoming and an
441	  outgoing flow, add conditions to the outgoing flows plus one `default`, add a
442	  matching join only if branches actually need synchronization, then re-waypoint
443	  the diagram (gateway shape + all edges).
444	- **Move logic into a subprocess**: move only elements that share a valid scope,
445	  re-scope their variables, recreate legal subprocess flow boundaries, and add a
446	  second diagram plane for the subprocess so nested content renders.
447	- **Add an entry point**: use a root-level start event, add a stable unique
448	  `uipath:entryPointId`, and declare input/output variables whose `elementId`
449	  matches that start event.
450	
451	Do not patch generated JSON to fix source behavior — change the `.bpmn` and
452	regenerate. For `Intsvc.*` activities/triggers, hand editing to CLI enrichment.
453	
454	### Edit red-flags
455	
456	Re-check after any edit:
457	
458	- A diagram plane references a missing process, collaboration, or subprocess.
459	- A rendered element lacks a shape or its flow lacks an edge/waypoint.
460	- An entry-point variable points at the wrong start event.
461	- A `uipath:context` value references a missing binding.
462	- A topology edit rewrote unrelated `uipath:*` extension XML.
463	- An Integration Service element carries hand-authored connection details.
464	
465	## Validation
466	
467	There is **no** `uip maestro bpmn validate` CLI command. The skill ships its own
468	offline validator that reconstructs the PO.Frontend Node/Edge/CanvasState model
469	from the parsed BPMN and runs **every** canvas validation rule — run it as the
470	primary check:
471	
472	```bash
473	cd skills/uipath-maestro-bpmn/validator && npm install --silent
474	node validate-bpmn.mjs <file.bpmn>   # prints VALID and exits 0, or prints errors and exits 1
475	```
476	
477	`VALID` / exit 0 means the document passes all rules. Any other output lists the
478	blocking errors (gateway/condition, fake-join, superfluous-gateway, error
479	end/boundary event, timer-duration, single-blank-start, variable-reference, and
480	IS-connector checks). See [validator/README.md](../validator/README.md).
481	
482	If Node is unavailable, fall back to a well-formed-XML parse plus the structural
483	checklist below — it mirrors the same blocking rules:
484	
485	```bash
486	python3 -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse('<file.bpmn>')"
487	```
488	
489	Then walk the structural checklist:
490	
491	1. Root is `<…:definitions>` with the BPMN + `uipath` namespaces.
492	2. Exactly one `<bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>` with a shape per node and an edge per flow.
493	3. Every `sourceRef`/`targetRef`/`attachedToRef`/`*Ref` resolves to a declared id.
494	4. Each XOR gateway: non-default flows have conditions; exactly one default.
495	5. No activity/event has more than one incoming flow.
496	6. Each event subprocess has exactly one start event, and it carries an event
497	   definition (with `isInterrupting`).
498	7. Every `vars.<id>` reference resolves to a declared variable.
499	8. Each `uipath:*` payload was produced from a `registry get` template, not
500	   hand-written.
501

1	# Expression Authoring
2	
3	Use this reference whenever pass 2 writes Maestro runtime expressions in BPMN
4	XML. Pass 1 may use business-readable placeholder conditions; pass 2 replaces
5	them with expressions only after the variables and scopes exist.
6	
7	## Stored expression shape
8	
9	- Use a leading `=` where Maestro expects expression content.
10	- Treat values without `=` as literals.
11	- Read BPMN variables through `vars.<variableId>`, for example
12	  `=vars.Var_RequestId`.
13	- Do not use bare variable names such as `=requestId` in generated runtime XML.
14	- Context bindings use `=bindings.<bindingId>`.
15	- Current element outputs use `result` only in output mappings for that
16	  element. Script task return values are exposed under `result.response`; use
17	  `source="=result.response"` for scalar returns or
18	  `source="=result.response.<field>"` for object fields.
19	- Multi-instance task bodies read the current item from `iterator.item`.
20	- Multi-instance subprocess bodies read the current item from
21	  `iterator[0].item`. Use `iterator[1].item` (and so on) inside nested
22	  multi-instance subprocesses: the index counts nesting depth from outermost
23	  (`[0]`) to innermost.
24	- The current 0-based loop index inside any multi-instance body is exposed as
25	  `iterator.loopCounter` for tasks and `iterator[N].loopCounter` for
26	  subprocesses at depth N.
27	- Error mapping conditions may inspect the built-in error object through
28	  `vars.error`, for example `=vars.error.code == "SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE"`.
29	
30	## Inline JavaScript with `=js:`
31	
32	When a mapping body or context value needs computation that a simple
33	`=vars.<id>` expression cannot express, prefix the body with `=js:` and follow
34	it with a JavaScript expression. The runtime evaluates the rest of the value
35	against the same `vars`, `bindings`, `result`, and `iterator` namespaces.
36	Use this form inside CDATA mapping bodies; it is the runtime-supported
37	escape hatch the BPMN expression grammar does not otherwise expose.
38	
39	```xml
40	<uipath:input name="JobArguments" type="json" target="bodyField"><![CDATA[
41	{"startRow":"=js:iterator[0].loopCounter * vars.Var_RowsPerShard",
42	 "endRow":"=js:(iterator[0].loopCounter + 1) * vars.Var_RowsPerShard - 1"}
43	]]></uipath:input>
44	```
45	
46	Rules:
47	
48	- The prefix is `=js:` (case-sensitive, no space).
49	- The body must still satisfy lint-sensitive constraints: no assignment
50	  operators in fields where read-only expressions are required.
51	- Prefer plain `=vars.<id>` or `=bindings.<id>` when the value does not need
52	  computation — `=js:` should be reserved for arithmetic, string
53	  manipulation, or conditional selection.
54	- A `=js:` expression that returns an object or array must produce valid JSON
55	  for fields typed `json`.
56	
57	Prefer JavaScript-safe variable ids such as `Var_RequestId`. If a brownfield
58	file contains non-identifier ids, preserve them and let the product editor or
59	CLI normalize the access form; do not silently rename variables without updating
60	all mappings, expressions, and generated metadata.
61	
62	## Lint-sensitive fields
63	
64	These fields must be read-only expressions:
65	
66	- Gateway `bpmn:conditionExpression` values.
67	- Activity skip conditions.
68	- Multi-instance completion and filter conditions.
69	- `uipath:errorMapping` condition values.
70	- Mapping values that read variables or element outputs.
71	
72	Do not use assignment operators in these fields. Comparisons such as `==`,
73	`===`, `!=`, `!==`, `>=`, and `<=` are allowed.
74	
75	## Scope and availability
76	
77	- Root variables are visible across the root process after they are declared and
78	  reachable by control flow.
79	- Subprocess variables stay scoped to that subprocess.
80	- Output mappings should target `uipath:inputOutput` or `uipath:output`
81	  variables, not read-only `uipath:input` variables.
82	- Entry point inputs that must later be updated need a separate mutable
83	  `uipath:inputOutput` variable and an explicit mapping from the entry input.
84	- Trigger-bound values are commonly represented as `uipath:inputOutput`
85	  variables scoped with `elementId` so the trigger can write them during
86	  execution.
87	
88	## Common mistakes
89	
90	- `=requestId` instead of `=vars.Var_RequestId`.
91	- `var="requestId"` instead of `var="Var_RequestId"`.
92	- Using `result` outside the output mapping of the element that produced it.
93	- Reading `iterator[0].item` outside the multi-instance subprocess body.
94	- Moving a variable into a subprocess without updating mappings that read it
95	  from the root scope.
96
